an3.115:1.1: “Monks, these three persons are to be found existing in the world.
an3.115:1.3: One who is easy to measure, one who is hard to measure, & one who is immeasurable.
an3.115:1.4: And which is the person who is easy to measure?
an3.115:1.5: There is the case where a certain person is high-strung, rowdy, unruly, talkative, of loose words & muddled mindfulness, unalert, unconcentrated, his mind scattered, his faculties left wide open.
an3.115:1.6: This is called a person who is easy to measure.
an3.115:2.1: And which is the person who is hard to measure?
an3.115:2.2: There is the case where a certain person is not high-strung, not rowdy, not unruly, not talkative, not of loose words—one with mindfulness established, alert, concentrated, his mind gathered into singleness, his faculties restrained.
an3.115:2.3: This is called a person who is hard to measure.
an3.115:3.1: And which is the person who is immeasurable?
an3.115:3.2: There is the case where a monk is an arahant, his effluents ended.
an3.115:3.3: This is called a person who is immeasurable.
an3.115:3.4: These, monks, are three persons to be found existing in the world.”
